title: Tanvi Patri, Abhinav Garg to Lead India at Junior Asia Championships
indian_angle: Two promising Indian juniors have been named to lead the national contingent at a major continental championship.
summary: Tanvi Patri and Abhinav Garg have been selected to spearhead the Indian team at the upcoming Badminton Asia Junior Championships.

title: India Finalizes Badminton Team for 2026 Asian Games
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has announced the official squad representing the nation at the prestigious 20th Asian Games.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has completed the finalization process for the national team that will compete in the 20th Asian Games in 2026.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game: Saina Nehwal or PV Sindhu Style?
indian_angle: This article delves into the playing style of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma, comparing her to two of India's greatest badminton icons.
summary: An analysis of Tanvi Sharma's evolving game suggests parallels with either Saina Nehwal's aggressive style or PV Sindhu's powerful play.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program Kicks Off in Guwahati, Raipur
indian_angle: A new program aimed at developing badminton coaches at the grassroots level has been launched in two key Indian cities.
summary: The National Coaching Excellence (NCE) centers in Guwahati and Raipur are hosting a Coaches Development Program focused on grassroots-level training from June 12th to 21st, 2026.

title: Indian Shuttlers Reveal FIFA World Cup 2026 Team Preferences
indian_angle: Top Indian badminton players share their favorite teams for the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026, offering a glimpse into their personal interests.
summary: Chirag Shetty and HS Prannoy favor Argentina, Ayush Shetty leans towards Spain, while PV Sindhu is considering Portugal or Germany for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

title: Ayush Shetty Defeats World No 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty secured a notable victory over a top-ranked Chinese opponent, showcasing his rising potential.
summary: Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty delivered a strong performance, giving no quarter to Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang.
